However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設}}/{{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉}}/{{ill|Chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Tokyo Onsens]] [[Category:Ōta Ward Geography]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]]

'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}'s {{ill|Ōta Ward|ja|大田区}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設}}/{{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉}}/{{ill|Chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Tokyo Onsens]] [[Category:Ōta Ward Geography]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]]

revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2024年10月}} {{出典の明記|date=2016年8月1日 (月) 20:16 (UTC)}} '''大田区の黒湯温泉'''（おおたくのくろゆおんせん）では、[[東京都]][[大田区]]に点在する[[黒色]]を呈した[[温泉]]（[[黒湯]]）について解説する。大田区内には[[2017年]]12月時点で20軒の温泉施設（[[銭湯]]など）があり、その数は[[東京都区部|東京23区]]内で最多である。 == 泉質 == [[源泉]]地により若干の差異はあるが、[[黒湯]]と呼ばれる温泉は、[[フミン酸]]などの[[有機化合物|有機物]]が含まれることによって[[透明度]]の低い（10cm程度）[[茶色|黒褐色]]の湯となり、大半が[[水素イオン指数|pH]]8〜9程度の弱[[塩基|アルカリ性]]になる傾向がある。なお、フミン酸の含有量によっては、3cm未満の極めて黒く低い透明度になることもある。[[ケイ酸|メタケイ酸]]や[[重曹]]類を多く含み、[[海]]因性の温泉とされることから、浴場によってはNaCl（[[塩化ナトリウム]]＝[[塩|食塩]]）、[[ヨウ素]]イオン、[[ホウ酸|メタほう酸]][[ホウ酸塩|塩]]などが含まれることもある。 [[療養泉]]における[[泉質]]の分類上は、[[炭酸水素塩泉]]や[[塩化物泉]]等に該当する。また、泉温は18[[℃]]程度の常温の冷[[鉱泉]]であるが、溶存成分により[[温泉#温泉法による温泉の定義|温泉法上の温泉規定]]に該当する。アルカリ性で肌がすべすべになることから、「[[炭酸水素塩泉#美肌効果|美人の湯]]」ともよばれている。ただし、アルカリ性であるために、同じアルカリである[[石鹸]]を多用すると[[皮膚]]の[[角質]]が臆弱化することもあり得る。 == 温泉と地域社会 == 温泉施設は[[蒲田]]周辺など[[商店街]]や[[住宅地]]の中に散在しており、[[温泉街]]と呼べるような地域は存在していない。その多くは温泉を利用した[[銭湯]]で、外観・設備や料金なども一般の銭湯と変わらないものが殆どである。 大田区役所や大田区商店街連合会は時折、黒湯の色にちなんだ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施し、区民の消費拡大や[[観光客]]誘致を図っている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}}。キャンペーンでは、隣接する[[川崎市|神奈川県川崎市]]と連携して商品化した[[地サイダー]]「黒湯サイダー」を販売することもある。 style="text-align:left"|下丸子エリアの温泉銭湯 |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ 第一相模湯]||西六郷2-29-2||－||－||ナトリウム-炭酸水素塩・塩化物冷鉱泉|| |- revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大田区の黒湯温泉]] to [[Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2024年10月}} {{出典の明記|date=2016年8月1日 (月) 20:16 (UTC)}} '''大田区の黒湯温泉'''（おおたくのくろゆおんせん）では、[[東京都]][[大田区]]に点在する[[黒色]]を呈した[[温泉]]（[[黒湯]]）について解説する。大田区内には[[2017年]]12月時点で20軒の温泉施設（[[銭湯]]など）があり、その数は[[東京都区部|東京23区]]内で最多である。 == 泉質 == [[源泉]]地により若干の差異はあるが、[[黒湯]]と呼ばれる温泉は、[[フミン酸]]などの[[有機化合物|有機物]]が含まれることによって[[透明度]]の低い（10cm程度）[[茶色|黒褐色]]の湯となり、大半が[[水素イオン指数|pH]]8〜9程度の弱[[塩基|アルカリ性]]になる傾向がある。なお、フミン酸の含有量によっては、3cm未満の極めて黒く低い透明度になることもある。[[ケイ酸|メタケイ酸]]や[[重曹]]類を多く含み、[[海]]因性の温泉とされることから、浴場によってはNaCl（[[塩化ナトリウム]]＝[[塩|食塩]]）、[[ヨウ素]]イオン、[[ホウ酸|メタほう酸]][[ホウ酸塩|塩]]などが含まれることもある。 [[療養泉]]における[[泉質]]の分類上は、[[炭酸水素塩泉]]や[[塩化物泉]]等に該当する。また、泉温は18[[℃]]程度の常温の冷[[鉱泉]]であるが、溶存成分により[[温泉#温泉法による温泉の定義|温泉法上の温泉規定]]に該当する。アルカリ性で肌がすべすべになることから、「[[炭酸水素塩泉#美肌効果|美人の湯]]」ともよばれている。ただし、アルカリ性であるために、同じアルカリである[[石鹸]]を多用すると[[皮膚]]の[[角質]]が臆弱化することもあり得る。 == 温泉と地域社会 == 温泉施設は[[蒲田]]周辺など[[商店街]]や[[住宅地]]の中に散在しており、[[温泉街]]と呼べるような地域は存在していない。その多くは温泉を利用した[[銭湯]]で、外観・設備や料金なども一般の銭湯と変わらないものが殆どである。 大田区役所や大田区商店街連合会は時折、黒湯の色にちなんだ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施し、区民の消費拡大や[[観光客]]誘致を図っている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}}。キャンペーンでは、隣接する[[川崎市|神奈川県川崎市]]と連携して商品化した[[地サイダー]]「黒湯サイダー」を販売することもある。 '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}'s {{ill|Ōta Ward|ja|大田区}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設}}/{{ill|Sento|ja|銭湯}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉}}/{{ill|Chloride spring|ja|塩化物泉|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{draft categories| }} {{Onsen}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Tokyo Onsens]] [[Category:Ōta Ward Geography]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=draft}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Ōta Ward|ja|大田区|12=draft}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=draft}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Tokyo Onsens]]
revision.text:
'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}'s {{ill|Ōta Ward|ja|大田区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Ward|qq=}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|qq=}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo
revision.text:
'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|qq=}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|qq=}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=Day-trip bathing facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip bathing facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Day-trip bathing facility" to "1-day onsen facility"
revision.text:
'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|qq=}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|qq=}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kuroyu Hot Spring in Ota Ward, Tokyo]] [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]]

As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
