Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q671405]] Wikidata name: Ōtaku no Kuroyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]'s [[:en:Ōta, Tokyo|Ōta Ward]]. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/[[:en:Sentō|Sento]] * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/[[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]] ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/{{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/{{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}'s {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|WD=Q217234|lt=Ōta Ward}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/{{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/{{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}'s {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|WD=Q217234|lt=Ōta Ward|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Humic acid|WD=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/{{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/{{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/{{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|WD=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/{{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]][[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] '''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}'s {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ōta Ward|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Humic acid|qid=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees. The Ōta Ward Office and local shopping street associations periodically conduct the "Ōta BLACK Campaign," named after the color of Kuroyu, aiming to boost local consumption and attract tourists.{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kanko/topics/ota-black-campain.html|title=「シン・ゴジラ」公開に合わせ「おおたBLACKキャンペーン」を実施します！|publisher=大田区役所|accessdate=2019-04-03}} The campaign occasionally features products such as "Kuroyu Cider," developed in collaboration with neighboring Kawasaki City. ==Major Hot Spring Facilities== {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left" |- !Facility Name!!Location!!Sauna!!Outdoor Bath!!Water Quality (Main Components)!!Notes |- !colspan="6"|Shimomaruko Area Hot Springs |- |[https://www.daiichisagamiyu.com/ Daiichi Sagamiyu]||Nishi-Rokugō 2-29-2||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate chloride cold mineral spring|| |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A7%80%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Hide no Yu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Shin-Kamata 3-26-1||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Natural mineral spring||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Oct. 31, 2019 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E6%96%B0%E7%94%B0%E6%B5%B4%E5%A0%B4 Shinden Yokujō]||Yaguchi 2-5-12||Free||-||Natural mineral spring|| |- !colspan="6"|Senzoku Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#fdd;"|[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E6%B9%AF Inariyu]||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Higashi-Yukigaya 3-30-14||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Yes||style="background-color:#fdd;"|-||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Bicarbonate soda, metasilicic acid||style="background-color:#fdd;"|Closed on Aug. 20, 2022 |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E8%AA%BF%E5%B8%83%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E6%B9%AF Chofu Benten Yu]||Kitamine-chō 10-11||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid||Light brown natural spring |- |[https://x.com/kugaharayu1010 Kugaharayu]||Kugahara 2-14-15||Steam free
Infrared ¥200||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring|| |- |[http://ota1010.com/explore/%E7%9B%8A%E3%81%AE%E6%B9%AF Masu no Yu]||Minami-Kugahara 2-1-23||Yes||-||Metasilicic acid, bicarbonate soda|| |- !colspan="6"|Ikegami Area Hot Springs |- |style="background-color:#dd7;"|[https://ota1010.com/explore/%e4%b9%85%e6%9d%be%e6%b8%a9%e6%b3%89/ Hisamatsu Onsen]||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Ikegami 3-31-16||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Free||style="background-color:#dd7;"|-||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||style="background-color:#dd7;"|Temporarily closed |- |[http://www.kaiseiyokujou.com Kaisei Yu]||Nishi-Kamata 5-10-5||-||-||Sodium bicarbonate cold spring||256mg humic substance, very dark Kuroyu. Flowing from the source. |} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * Ōta Sento Map – Available at Ōta Tourism Association and various ward offices. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor Hot Spring|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor Hot Spring|1=Moor hot springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11344950}} * {{ill|Tsunashima Onsen|ja|綱島温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunashima Onsen|1=Tsunashima Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11255699}} * {{ill|Day-trip hot spring facilities|ja|日帰り温泉施設|1=1-day onsen facility|2=ja|3=日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-trip hot spring facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}/{{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|qid=Q1046648|lt=Sento|1=Sentō}} * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate spring|qq=}}/{{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} ==External Links== * [http://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/seikatsu/hoken/eisei/riyoubiyou/ootakunoonnsenn.html Ōta Ward Onsen (Official)] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|大田区の黒湯温泉|version=102284341|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大田区の黒湯温泉]] [[fa:چشمه آبگرم منطقه اوتا]] {{wikidata link|Q671405}} [[Category:Geography of Ōta, Tokyo]] [[Category:Hot springs of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kuroyu]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

'''Kuroyu Onsen in Ōta Ward''' (大田区の黒湯温泉) describes the black-colored hot springs ({{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}) scattered throughout {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}'s {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ōta Ward|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}. As of December 2017, Ōta Ward had 20 hot spring facilities (including public bathhouses), the highest number among Tokyo's 23 special wards. ==Spring quality== While there are slight differences depending on the source, the hot springs known as Kuroyu contain organic substances like {{ill|humic acid|ja|フミン酸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/humic acid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Humic acid|qid=Q224896}}, resulting in dark brown water with low transparency (about 10 cm visibility). Most of these springs tend to have a mildly alkaline pH of around 8 to 9. Depending on the humic acid concentration, some springs can have transparency of less than 3 cm, appearing extremely dark. They contain high amounts of metasilicic acid and bicarbonate compounds and are generally considered marine-origin springs, sometimes containing sodium chloride (common salt), iodine ions, and metaboric acid salts. In therapeutic hot spring classifications, these springs typically fall under bicarbonate springs and chloride springs. The temperature of the springs is around 18°C, making them cold mineral springs by nature. However, their dissolved mineral content qualifies them as hot springs under the Hot Spring Act. Due to their alkalinity, these waters are known as "Bijin no Yu" (beauty-enhancing waters), making the skin smooth. However, excessive use of alkaline soap can weaken the skin’s outer layer. ==Hot Springs and Local Society== Most hot spring facilities are scattered within shopping streets and residential areas around Kamata, without a distinct hot spring district. Many facilities operate as regular public bathhouses (sento) with typical appearances, facilities, and fees.
